about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: AlgoRythmics transforms algorithm learning into an engaging, musical experience. We combine dance, music, and interactive technology to make complex concepts intuitive and fun.
about.mission_text1: We believe that algorithms don't have to be intimidating. Through the universal language of music and movement, we make sorting and searching algorithms accessible to everyone — from first-year CS students to curious learners of all ages.
about.mission_text2: Our platform combines folk dance traditions from around the world with algorithm visualizations, creating a unique and memorable learning experience that bridges culture and technology.
about.team_subtitle: Meet the passionate people behind AlgoRythmics

01.title: Choose an Algorithm
01.desc: Select from our comprehensive library of sorting, searching, and backtracking algorithms to explore.

02.title: Select Learning Mode
02.desc: Pick your preferred way to learn — watch videos, interact with animations, or take manual control.

03.title: Learn & Practice
03.desc: Understand the algorithm step-by-step with visual aids, music, and dance choreography.

04.title: Create & Share
04.desc: Build your own inputs, test edge cases, and share your learning progress with the community.

about.cta_subtitle: Join thousands of students who have discovered the joy of learning algorithms through music and dance.

algorithms.inspiring_text: Algorithms are not just dry lines of code, they are the perfect harmony of logic and movement. Discover the rhythm of programming, and master the fundamentals of computer science where the magical power of music and dance helps you understand the invisible.

empty.adjustSearchOrFilters: Try adjusting your search or category filters.

cta.title: Already mastered the basics?
cta.subtitle: Take the full journey.
cta.description: Check out our structured courses and interactive workshops to master algorithmic thinking.

detail.redo_confirm: This step is already completed. Redoing it will overwrite your previous results. Do you want to restart?

video: Simple explanation: elements are compared in pairs and swapped if they are in the wrong order.
Watch how the dancers move to the rhythm; every move represents a logical step.
The goal is to let the largest elements 'bubble up' to the end of the array.
animation: Follow the automatic movement of the visual bars on the screen.
Colors help distinguish between comparisons and swap operations.
Help: Use the control buttons to adjust speed or manually step through the process.
control: Take an active role in sorting! Click two adjacent elements to compare them.
Decide at each step whether to swap the elements or keep them as they are.
Help: If you get stuck, click the lightbulb icon to see the next correct move.
create: Type the missing parts of the code manually to complete the algorithm.
Press Enter or click the check icon to verify your answer.
Help: Click the 'I Need Help' button to see multiple-choice options for the selected field.
alive: Write the algorithm completely on your own! Use your coding logic to solve the task.
Pay attention to variable names and loop boundaries for correct software execution.
Help: Switch to block mode if you want to assemble the logic units visually.

algorithms.title: All algorithms

bubble-sort.name: Bubble sort
bubble-sort.description: Bubble sort's goal is to move the largest elements one by one to the end of the list (like rising bubbles), comparing and swapping adjacent items in pairs.
steps: Compare adjacent elements
Swap if they are in the wrong order
Move to the next pair
Repeat until no swaps are needed
The list is now sorted

insertion-sort.name: Insertion sort
insertion-sort.description: Insertion sort works like sorting playing cards in your hand: each new element is inserted into its correct position within the already sorted portion.
steps: Start with the second element
Compare with elements to its left
Shift larger elements right
Insert the element in its correct position
Repeat for all remaining elements

selection-sort.name: Selection sort
selection-sort.description: Selection sort finds the smallest element from the unsorted part in each pass and moves it to the beginning of the sorted part.
steps: Find the minimum element in the unsorted region
Swap it with the first unsorted element
Expand the sorted region by one
Repeat until the entire array is sorted

shell-sort.name: Shell sort
shell-sort.description: A sorting algorithm proposed by Donald Shell in 1959, it is a variant of straight insertion sort that allows records to take long leaps rather than single steps.
steps: Choose a gap sequence
Sort elements separated by the gap using insertion sort
Reduce the gap
Repeat until the gap is 1
Perform final insertion sort pass

merge-sort.name: Merge sort
merge-sort.description: Merge sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that divides the array in half, sorts each half, and then merges them back together in the correct order.
steps: Divide the array into two halves
Recursively sort each half
Merge the sorted halves
Compare elements from each half
Place the smaller element into the result

quick-sort.name: Quick sort
quick-sort.description: Quick sort selects a pivot element and partitions the array around it, placing smaller elements before and larger after the pivot, then recursively sorts both sides.
steps: Choose a pivot element
Partition: elements < pivot go left, > pivot go right
Recursively sort the left partition
Recursively sort the right partition
Combine the results

heap-sort.name: Heap sort
heap-sort.description: Heap sort uses a binary heap data structure. It builds a max-heap from the array, then repeatedly extracts the maximum element to build the sorted array.
steps: Build a max-heap from the array
Extract the root (maximum) element
Move it to the end of the array
Heapify the reduced heap
Repeat until all elements are sorted

linear-search.name: Linear search
linear-search.description: A sequential search algorithm that checks each element of the array from the beginning, comparing each element with the desired value.
steps: Start from the first element
Compare with the target
If match, return position
If no match, move to the next element
If end of list, target not found

binary-search.name: Binary search
binary-search.description: An efficient search algorithm that repeatedly divides the sorted search interval in half until the target value is found or the interval is empty.
steps: Ensure the array is sorted
Find the middle element
Compare with the target
If target is smaller, search the left half
If target is larger, search the right half

n-queens.name: N-Queens problem
n-queens.description: The N-Queens problem is a classic backtracking problem that involves placing N chess queens on an N×N chessboard so that no two queens threaten each other.
steps: Place a queen in the first row
Move to the next row and try each column
Check if placement is safe (no conflicts)
If no safe column exists, backtrack
Repeat until all queens are placed

bogosort.name: Bogosort
bogosort.description: Bogosort is an intentionally inefficient sorting algorithm that randomly shuffles the array until it's sorted by pure chance. Hilarious in dance!
steps: Check if the list is sorted
If not sorted, shuffle the list randomly
Repeat until the list is sorted by chance

privacy_page.title: Privacy Policy
privacy_page.gen_text: At AlgoRythmics, your privacy is a priority. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.
privacy_page.data_text: We collect the following types of data:
data_list: Profile information: first name, last name, email address;
Progress data: time spent on the platform, completed lessons, and exercise results;
Cookies: to improve the user experience and ensure account security.
privacy_page.usage_text: We use the collected data to:
usage_list: Provide access to interactive courses and exercises;
Improve educational content based on user feedback.
privacy_page.protection_text: We implement technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, alteration, or destruction.
privacy_page.rights_text: You have the right to access, rectify, or delete your personal data. You can delete your account at any time directly from the 'Profile' section.
